content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1109 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest

(916) 322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

August 30, 1978

78-08-082

Mr. Jerry Rapp
Bristol Myers Co.
1155 - 15th St. N.W., Suite 606
Washington, D.C. 20005

Dear Mr. Rapp:

     This letter confirms our recent telephone con-
versation concerning a political awareness program which
Bristol Myers intends to initiate at its Clairol facility
in California. Although the program is still in the
planning stage, you assured me that the activities will
be nonpartisan and will comply with FEC regulations.

     As I told you at that time, California law does
not prohibit corporations from engaging in political
activities. Moreover, since the activities you describe
will be nonpartisan, Bristol Myers will not have any
reporting obligations under California law by virtue of
sponsoring candidates' nights, voter registration, infor-
mation seminars and the other activities you described.

     I hope this information will be of assistance.

                              Sincerely,

                              [signature]
                              Natalie E. West
                              Legal Counsel
                              Legal Division

NEW:plh

29

State of California

Memorandum

To : Opinion Request Participants                    Date : 8/25/78


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Natalie West

Subject: Reportability of "political awareness" activities


       Bristol Myers is developing an experimental
"political awareness" program which it plans to
implement at its Clairol Plant in California. The
program will be unrelated to the company PAC and will
comply with FEC regulations.

       The company plans to sponsor voter registration
and a candidates' forum, to put up posters urging
employees to vote, to conduct seminars telling employees
how to obtain absentee ballots and so forth.

       I have been assured that these activities will
be non-partisan. Projected expenses include costs of
letterhead, brochures, posters, and donations to
League of Women Voters or some other non-profit organ-
ization that federal law apparently requires to co-
sponsor the program.

       Is this non-reportable?




[illegible signature]
